title: Align MS in Data Science - Khoury College of Computer Sciences
text:
Align MS in Data Science - Khoury College of Computer Sciences
Align MS in Data Science
Apply now
Request info
Today, all companies are driven by data and the demand for expertise in data science has never been higher. The Align MS in Data Science will teach you the skills to harness the power of data, even without a tech background. Study with a supportive cohort of your peers as you spend the first two semesters building a solid CS foundation. Then, you’ll dive deep into the world of data, learning to use it to solve some of the world’s most complex organizational problems.
Explore program requirements in the course catalog
Program highlights
Semester hours
44 total semester hours required
Start term
Fall, Spring (spring varies depending on location)
Program modality
In person
Career prospects and top employers
In the digital age, every industry relies on data to stay competitive. Companies of all sizes across the country are actively looking to fill thousands of data-driven roles. Our graduates find success in a variety of companies such as Walmart, Meta, Google, CVS Health, Dell, Fidelity Investments, Microsoft, McKinsey & Company, Tesla, ACLU, Massachusetts General Hospital, and more in careers that include:
Data scientist
Machine learning engineer
Software engineer
Data engineer
AI consultant
32% of our graduates
have gone on to data scientist or senior data scientist roles.
What makes Align different
Learn about the successful collaboration between Amazon Web Services (AWS) and Align students, and what makes it different: “We’re not just bringing in trained engineers. We’re bringing a set of folks who have a different set of perspectives, a different set of experiences. They bring a fresh understanding — a fresh set of eyes,” says Wayne Duso, former AWS vice president.
How the Align program works
Learning outcomes
The Align MS in Data Science will equip you with technical knowledge and career preparation. As a a graduate, you will have gained proficiency in:
Data management:
Ability to effectively utilize integrated databases for managing large-scale datasets.
Machine learning:
Ability to effectively apply an appropriate machine-learning model to a problem.
Analysis and visualization:
Ability to effectively communicate insights derived from data.
Did you know?
The average base salary for data scientists in the US is $122,928
Indeed, August 2024
Bridging the gap: the Align advantage
Learn about Align student LeAnn’s experience entering the program with a non-tech undergraduate degree, and how the Align program has given her a way to break into the field.
Campus locations
Portland
The collaborative nature of Northeastern’s Roux Institute in Portland connects students with corporate and governmental power players across the Northeast, and beyond.
Explore Portland's campus
Silicon Valley
Located in the heart of the tech revolution (and where it began), the San Jose campus offers students the opportunities to advance the CS field in a program known for industry connections and diverse work opportunities.
Explore Silicon Valley's campus
Boston
Located in the heart of Boston, Northeastern’s oldest campus is home to a vibrant community of students, researchers, and faculty working across disciplines to build a better world through CS.
Explore Boston's campus
Applications and admissions
How to apply
How to apply
Our Align Apply page has everything you need to know about applying to the Align MS in Computer Science program at Khoury college, including:
Align admissions requirements
Timelines and deadlines
Resources and contact information
Learn about applying to Align
Degree costs
Degree costs
Visit Student Financial Services for current tuition and fee information, and to find tools and calculators to help you estimate the cost of your degree, based on 44 required semester hours.
Visit Student Financial Services
Financial support
Financial support
Pursuing a master’s degree is a significant investment in your future. Northeastern University and Khoury College of Computer Sciences are here to help support you in any way we can. From financial aid to scholarship opportunities to co-op placements and teaching assistantships, there are multiple options to help fund your degree as you work toward your goals.
Learn more about financial support
Deadlines
Domestic
Fall semester: August 1
Spring semester: December 1
International
Fall semester: April 15
Spring semester: September 15
Meet our faculty
Get to know the engaged, experienced professors who are here to support you every step of the way.
View all faculty
Kylie Ariel Bemis
Kylie Ariel Bemis is an assistant teaching professor at Khoury College, who develops the master’s in data science program curriculum. She is interested in statistical computing environments and methods for complex data, and active in outreach to the Native American and LGBTQ+ communities.
Read bio
